title: Scene node snapping and selection bounds calculation
purpose: Provides utilities for calculating snap distances and selection boundaries for scene nodes during interactive editing.
module_context: Exports constants for snap threshold and interfaces for snap guides and results. Defines functions for computing snap adjustments and selection bounds.

methods.name: getEdges
methods.normalized_params: (node: SceneNode): { left: number; right: number; centerX: number; top: number; bottom: number; centerY: number }

steps.content: Calculate edge positions for a node with zero rotation.
IF the node has a non-zero rotation, compute rotated corner positions.
Determine the bounding rectangle of the rotated corners.
Return the calculated edge positions including center coordinates.

methods.name: computeSnap
methods.normalized_params: (movingIds: Set<string>, movingBounds: Rect, allNodes: SceneNode[]): SnapResult

steps.content: Filter out nodes that are currently moving from the list of all nodes.
IF no target nodes remain, return zero snap adjustments and empty guides.
Calculate edge positions for the moving nodes.
For each target node, evaluate potential x-axis snap positions.
For each target node, evaluate potential y-axis snap positions.
Track the best snap adjustments and collect guide information.
Return the computed snap adjustments and guide information.
IF no nodes are provided, return null for bounds.
Initialize boundary coordinates with extreme values.
For each node, calculate its edge positions.
Update the boundary coordinates based on each node's edges.
Return the calculated bounding rectangle for the selection.
